documents.title: Headstage
documents.text: The KonteX X-Headstage™ series is a family of high-performance neural recording interfaces designed to provide stable, low-noise signal acquisition across a wide range of experimental configurations.
documents.tags: neural-recording-headstages, kontex-x-headstage-series, headstage-designs-for-neuroscience, high-performance-neural-recording, experimental-configurations-for-brain-computer-interface, low-noise-signal-acquisition, neural-interfaces-for-research, stable-electrical-signals-in-brain, brain-machine-interfaces-with-kontex

documents.title: Headstage
documents.text: Engineered for both head-fixed and freely moving animal studies, X-Headstages combine compact mechanical design, flexible connectivity, and scalable channel configurations to support modern electrophysiology research.
documents.tags: Neurotechnology, FAE Expert, Headstage, Electrophysiology Research, Animal Studies, Neural Interface, Neuroscientific Equipment, Brain Machine Interface, Electrophysiology Hardware, Neuroscience Devices

documents.title: Headstage
documents.text: The series is fully compatible with the XDAQ acquisition platform and supports integration with multiple probe technologies and adapter ecosystems.
documents.tags: neurotechnology, neural interfaces, headstages, xdaq acquisition platform, probe technologies, adapter ecosystems, brain-machine interfaces, neurophysiology, data acquisition systems, electrophysiology equipment.

documents.title: XeR16-HDMI-ACC
documents.text: E-series X-Headstage™ is designed to offer the best value while maintaining the flexibility of the X-Headstage™ that can be used with several off-the-shelf probe adapters. The advantage of adapter based approach can increase the utilization and longevity of headstage usage. If the probe facing connector breaks due to daily wear, it can be easily and cheaply replaced.
documents.tags: XeR16-HDMI-ACC, Neurotechnology Hardware, FAE Expertise, X-Headstage Specifications, Probe Adapter Compatibility, Headstage Replacement Policy, HDMI Connectivity Options, Analog-to-Digital Conversion, Multi-Channel Data Acquisition.

documents.title: XeR16-HDMI-ACC, Motion Tracking
documents.text: All XR Headstages are equipped with a built-in analog 3-axis accelerometer as a standard feature. The accelerometer is sampled at the same rate as the headstage input channels, and its data are available through the AUX1, AUX2, and AUX3 channels in the recorded data stream. The analog 3-axis accelerometer is included with all XR Headstages and cannot be omitted.
documents.tags: accelerometer, motion tracking, xr headset, hdi, xer16, neurotechnology, fae, headstage, aux channels

documents.title: XeR16-HDMI-ACC, Motion Tracking
documents.text: For applications requiring active cable rotation compensation, an optional IMU Sensor is available. When used in conjunction with a KonteX Commutator, the IMU Sensor provides real-time rotational motion data that allows the Commutator to automatically rotate according to the animal's movement, minimizing cable twisting during freely moving experiments. The IMU Sensor is intended for Commutator-based motion compensation and is not part of the standard headstage configuration.
documents.tags: Neurotechnology, FAE Expert, HDMI Active Cable, Motion Tracking System, IMU Sensor, KonteX Commutator, Active Cabling Compensation, Animal Research Equipment, Neural Headstage Design.

documents.title: XeR16-HDMI-ACC, Motion Tracking

documents.title: X3SR32-HDMI
documents.text: The X3SR32 is a 32-channel stim-record X-Headstage™ developed by KONTEX, designed for simultaneous neural stimulation and recording in electrophysiological research. Based on two RHS2116 chips from Intan Technologies, the X3SR32 delivers reliable bidirectional data acquisition and output performance in a compact and robust form factor.
documents.tags: Neurotechnology, FAE expert, KONTEX X3SR32-HDMI, Intan Technologies RHS2116, neural stimulation, electrophysiology research, X-Headstage, 32-channel stim-record, high-speed data acquisition., simultaneous stimulation and recording in rat motor cortex

documents.title: X3SR32-HDMI, Armor
documents.text: Optional protective covers are available for each headstage, offering extra protection and anchoring. These covers, crafted from Polyoxymethylene (POM), are lightweight and resistant to chemicals. There are three standard armor types, with options for customization in shape and material to suit diverse experimental requirements. It's important to note that the size, weight, and shape of these armors will differ across various X-Headstage™ models.
documents.tags: X3SR32-HDMI headstage protection, Polyoxymethylene armor, Neurotechnology headstages, FAE protective covers, Experimental neuroscience hardware, Headstage anchoring systems, Electrophysiology equipment, Neuroscience research tools, Brain-machine interface accessories, Neurostimulation devices.

documents.title: X3R64-HDMI, Armor
documents.text: Optional protective covers are available for each headstage, offering extra protection and anchoring. These covers, crafted from Polyoxymethylene (POM), are lightweight and resistant to chemicals. There are three standard armor types, with options for customization in shape and material to suit diverse experimental requirements. It's important to note that the size, weight, and shape of these armors will differ across various X-Headstage™ models.

documents.title: X6R128-HDMI
documents.text: The X6R line is KonteX’s high-channel-count X-Headstage™, engineered for advanced neural recording applications requiring 128 channels or more. Despite its high channel density, the X6R maintains a compact form factor, approximately 10 mm x 10 mm and only a few millimeters thick, making it ideal for space-constrained experimental setups.
documents.tags: X6R128-HDMI, Neural Recording Headstage, High-Channel-Count Neurotechnology, X-Headstage, KonteX Hardware, Neural Interface, HDMI Connectivity, Neural Signal Acquisition, High-Density EEG, chronic electrophysiology recording with a high channel count headstage

documents.title: X6R128-HDMI, Armor
documents.text: Optional protective covers are available for each headstage, offering extra protection and anchoring. These covers, crafted from Polyoxymethylene (POM), are lightweight and resistant to chemicals. There are three standard armor types, with options for customization in shape and material to suit diverse experimental requirements. It's important to note that the size, weight, and shape of these armors will differ across various X-Headstage™ models.
documents.tags: Neurotechnology, FAE, Headstage, Armor, Polyoxymethylene, POM, Protective Covers, Anchoring, Chemical Resistance, Experimental Requirements

documents.title: System Adapter
documents.text: System Adapters are designed to enable seamless integration between KONTEX acquisition hardware and external systems, accessories, or signal interfaces. These adapters provide flexible connectivity options, allowing researchers to bridge different connector standards, expand system compatibility, and integrate additional devices into their experimental setups.
documents.tags: Neurotechnology, FAE Expertise, System Adapter, Acquisition Hardware Interface, Connector Standard Bridge, Signal Integration Module, External System Connectivity, Seamless Data Transfer, Multisystem Compatibility Adapter, Interoperability Solutions

documents.title: SAdpt-XDAQ-Smartlink

documents.title: SAdpt-XDAQ-OM12
documents.text: The XDAQ™ to Intan RHD Headstage Adapter is designed to interface the XDAQ acquisition system with Intan’s RHD headstages via SPI cables. This adapter provides a direct and reliable connection path, enabling precise recording control while maintaining compatibility with Intan’s standard hardware architecture.
documents.tags: bridges a third-party Intan-brand headstage into the KonteX ecosystem, cross-vendor interoperability with a competing manufacturer's hardware

documents.title: SAdpt-XDAQ-OM12, Usage Scenarios
documents.text: The SAdpt-XDAQ-OM12 adapter provides a direct and reliable interface between Intan RHD headstages and the XDAQ system. It can be used in experimental setups where stable high- channel-count recording is required, and it also supports extended configurations through optional SPI cables, allowing flexible positioning of the headstages relative to the acquisition system.
documents.tags: Neurotechnology, FAE, Intan RHD Headstages, XDAQ System Adapter, Data Acquisition Hardware, Neurophysiology Recording Equipment, Brain Machine Interface, High Channel Count Recording, Neural Signal Acquisition.

documents.title: SAdpt-XDAQ-OM12, Usage Scenarios
documents.text: The HDMI slot on the board allows the adaptor to be plugged directly into the XDAQ acquisition box. Both the P1 and P2 connectors on the adaptor can also be used with SPI cable to extend the connection to the acquisition system.
documents.tags: Neurotechnology, FAE Expertise, High-Speed Data Acquisition, XDAQ Acquisition Box, HDMI Connectivity, SPI Interface, Adaptor Board Technology, Digital Signal Processing, Industrial Computing Hardware

documents.title: SyncLink
documents.text: The primary purpose of the SyncLink is to enable precise time-step synchronization between multiple XDAQ systems. This is especially critical when performing simultaneous XR and XSR experiments on the same subject, which require the use of separate XDAQ units.
documents.tags: Neurotechnology hardware, Time-step synchronization, FAE expert support, XDAQ system integration, SyncLink technology, Experimental setup configuration, Precision timing requirements, High-speed data acquisition, Multi-unit synchronization solution, Neuroscientific research equipment, the SyncLink do when I have multiple XDAQ systems, experiments across multiple rigs are drifting out of sync

documents.title: SyncLink
documents.text: By synchronizing multiple XDAQs, the SyncLink ensures that data acquired from different headstage types are time-aligned, enabling accurate integration and analysis across systems.
documents.tags: Neurotechnology Expertise, FAE Expertise, High-Speed Data Synchronization, Time-Aligned Data Acquisition, Headstage Interfacing, Neuroscientific Research Equipment, XDAQ Synchronization Protocol, Brain-Computer Interface Technology, Neural Signal Processing, Real-Time Data Integration, synchronizing two separate recording rigs in different rooms

documents.title: SyncLink, Device Overview
documents.text:
① Sync Ports (IN / OUT) :
Each SyncLink is equipped with Sync IN and Sync OUT ports.
SyncLinks are interconnected using standard Ethernet cables.
The IN / OUT configuration allows synchronization signals to be distributed across multiple SyncLinks in a daisy-chain.
documents.tags: Ethernet synchronization, SyncLink device, Synchronization signal distribution, Daisy chain setup, Network interconnectivity, Signal synchronization technology, Neurotechnology hardware, FAE technical documentation, Interconnected sync ports, Sync IN/OUT port configuration.

documents.title: SyncLink, Device Overview
documents.text:
② Harp Port :
A 3.5 mm audio jack is provided for connection to the Harp Clock Synchronizer.
An adjacent status LED indicates the operational state of the Harp Port.
documents.tags: Neurotechnology hardware, FAE, Technical documentation, SyncLink device, Harp Port specifications, Audio jack interface, Status LED indicators, Device synchronization technology, Neurointerface hardware

documents.title: SyncLink, Device Overview
documents.text:
③ Expansion Port :
The Expansion Port is used to connect a KonteX IO Expander, allowing extended I/O functionality as required by the experimental setup.
documents.tags: Neurotechnology hardware, expansion port connectivity, KonteX IO Expander compatibility, experimental setup integration, SyncLink device features, digital signal processing, I/O expansion modules, advanced input output functions, FAE technical documentation, neuroscientific research equipment.

documents.title: SyncLink, Device Overview
documents.text:
④ XDAQ Expansion Ports :
EXP1/2 (HDMI): Connects the SyncLink to an XDAQ system.
These ports enable synchronization signal exchange between the SyncLink and connected XDAQ units.
documents.tags: Neurotechnology hardware documentation, FAE support, SyncLink device overview, XDAQ expansion ports, HDMI connectivity, synchronization signal exchange, Neurotech hardware experts, XDAQ system integration, expansion port technology

documents.title: SyncLink, Device Overview
documents.text:
⑤ Ground Ports :
SYS GND (4 mm) — The digital ground shared by the internal electronic circuitry.
Chassis GND (4 mm) — Connected to the metal enclosure, the outer shield of the HDMI cable, and earth ground via the power supply.
As a starting point, we recommend bonding SYS GND and Chassis GND together and using this common node to ground the Faraday cage shielding. Since environmental conditions can vary significantly, users should evaluate and adjust the grounding configuration to determine the most effective setup for their specific environment.
documents.tags: Neurotechnology, FAE Expertise, Grounding Configuration, Shielding Techniques, Faraday Cage Design, Electrical Noise Reduction, EMI Suppression, EMS Control Systems, Electromagnetic Interference Solutions

documents.title: SAdpt-Port-EXP
documents.text: The Port Expander is designed for researchers looking to maximize the number of animals monitored by the XDAQ system. It allows each HDMI headstage port in the XDAQ to support up to four animals with XR Headstages or up to two animals with XSR (stim-record) Headstages. Please refer to the XDAQ model and headstage compatibility table for more details.
documents.tags: record from more animals, increase number of animals monitored simultaneously, scale up experiment, expand headstage ports, independent isolated grounding per animal, monitor more animals than my XDAQ headstage ports allow, increase the number of animals I can record from at once

documents.title: SAdpt-Port-EXP
documents.text: The Port Expander conditions the control signal and provides isolated and dedicated electrical systems to each connected headstage. As a result, the ground and reference nodes from each animal's headstage do not interfere with one another.
documents.tags: scales a setup to many subjects at once, per-subject isolation when running several animals together, grows the animal throughput of one system

documents.title: SAdpt-Port-EXP, Cable and Commutator Compatibility
documents.text: The Port Expander is compatible with all KonteX cable and commutator solutions. For optimal performance, it is recommended to use the same cable configuration and commutator model for all connected headstages.
documents.tags: Neurotechnology, FAE Expert, Port Expanders, Cable Compatibility, Commutator Solutions, Headstage Connectivity, Neurostimulation Devices, Medical Electronics, Electrophysiology Equipment, Brain Computer Interface

documents.title: SAdpt-XDAQ-OM16
documents.text: The XDAQ™ to Intan RHS Headstage Adapter is designed to interface the XDAQ acquisition system with Intan’s RHS headstages via Stim SPI cables. This adapter provides a direct and reliable connection path, enabling precise stimulation and recording control while maintaining compatibility with Intan’s standard hardware architecture.
documents.tags: Neurotechnology, FAE, Intan RHS Headstage Adapter, XDAQ Acquisition System, Stim SPI Cables, Neurostimulation Interface, Neural Recording Hardware, Electrophysiology Equipment, FEE (Front End Electronics) Compatibility

documents.title: SAdpt-XDAQ-OM16, Usage Scenarios
documents.text: The SAdpt-XDAQ-OM16 adapter provides a direct and reliable interface between Intan RHS headstages and the XDAQ system. It can be used in experimental setups where stable high- channel-count recording is required, and it also supports extended configurations through optional Stim SPI cables, allowing flexible positioning of the headstages relative to the acquisition system.
documents.tags: Neurotechnology adapter, XDAQ interface, Intan RHS headstage, Neural data acquisition, High-channel-count recording, Neurostimulation, FAE documentation, Adapter for neural recording, Neuroscientific research equipment.

documents.title: SAdpt-SR64
documents.text: The SR64 Adapter is essential for the proper operation of the X3SR64 Headstage. It features active electronic circuitry that conditions and merges headstage communication, which typically requires two separate HDMI cables. Unlike the standard X3SR32 headstage, which uses LVDS signal lines that require double the number of signal pins, the SR64 Adapter converts LVDS signal pairs into single-ended CMOS signals.
documents.tags: Neurotechnology, FA-Electronics, Headstage-Adapter, Signal-Conversion-Circuitry, HDMI-Splitter, FAE-Expert, X3SR64-Documentation, Neurotech-Hardware, Analog-to-Digital-Conversion, headstage isn't compatible with my current XDAQ ports

documents.title: SAdpt-SR64
documents.text: This allows it to drive four RHS2116 chips, which make up the X3SR64 headstage, using a single KonteX HDMI cable. The adapter has been meticulously designed and tested to ensure the reliable performance of the X3SR64 Headstage.
documents.tags: consolidates several amplifier chips onto a single cable run, reduces cable count for a high channel density headstage

documents.title: SAdpt-SR64, Dimension and Weight

documents.title: Short Circuit Analyzer
documents.text: The Hardware Short Circuit Analyzer is a high-performance device engineered for efficient and precise short circuit testing, supporting up to 256-channel combinations. This analyzer is designed to identify and diagnose short circuits across large-scale circuits and complex electrode configurations, making it ideal for use in electronic design verification, fault troubleshooting, and quality control processes.

documents.title: Commutator
documents.text: KonteX offers both passive and motorized commutator designs to support diverse electrophysiological paradigms.Engineered for minimal breakaway torque and seamless multi-modal integration, our commutators deliver high performance and usability while providing exceptional value for advanced neuroscience research.
documents.tags: Neurotechnology, FAE, Commutator Design, Electrophysiology, Neurosensing, Neuroscience Research, Electroencephalography, High-Performance Electronics, Advanced Neurostimulation Systems, stimulation and recording in awake rodents with tethered cables that must not tangle

documents.title: Commutator
documents.text: Our newly redesigned non-motorized commutator delivers enhanced signal quality and low friction operation that are improved over the previous generation. It features integrated animal-side cable management for secure mounting and convenient cable storage. This versatile design supports both XR (recording only, up to 256 channels per cable) and XSR (stimulation-recording, up to 32 channels per cable) headstages, providing a streamlined solution for diverse experimental needs.
documents.tags: Neurotechnology hardware, FAE documentation, Commutator technology, Low friction commutators, Signal quality enhancement, Cable management systems, Animal-side mounting solutions, Neurostimulation recording devices, Electrophysiology equipment, Neuroresearch hardware components

documents.title: Commutator
documents.text: The Commutator PRO is our most advanced and highly customizable model, featuring a built-in low-noise motor and two flexible torque sensing options: an animal-side gyroscope or an integrated Hall sensor. Its onboard microprocessor continuously and instantaneously detects and compensates for cable torque generated by animal movement, ensuring stable recordings and naturalistic behavior.
documents.tags: Neurotechnology, FAE Expert, Commutator PRO, Low-Noise Motor, Torque Sensing Options, Gyroscope, Hall Sensor, Animal Behavior Recording, Cable Management System, Neurophysiology Equipment

documents.title: Commutator PRO
documents.text: The Commutator Pro is a high-performance, motorized commutator designed to prevent tether tangling during neuroscience experiments, ensuring smooth and uninterrupted behavioral recordings. It can operate using external power or its integrated, rechargeable lithium battery, offering flexible deployment options. A built-in microprocessor, working in tandem with a rotary magnetic sensor and/ or an external gyroscopic sensor, actively manages cable orientation by automatically counteracting twists.
documents.tags: Neuroscience equipment, motorized commutator, tether management system, behavioral recording equipment, neuroscience experiment hardware, electromechanical device, motorized cable management, research equipment for neuroscience, laboratory automation, neuroscience data acquisition, keep tangling when the animal moves freely, what do I need

documents.title: Commutator PRO
documents.text: The system is highly configurable, supporting up to four Neuropixels ports alongside a conventional probe driven by either recording or stim-record headstages, and offers up to nine GPIO channels for user-defined applications. Its adaptable mounting and cable management system, complemented by an optional passthrough channel, enables seamless integration across a wide range of experimental setups—providing robust performance and maximum versatility.
documents.tags: runs several independent recording channels through one rotating interface at once, multi-probe throughput on a single device

documents.title: Commutator PRO, Step 1: Powering the commutator
documents.text: The Commutator Pro can be powered via either the USB-C port or the DC jack. When using the USB-C port—particularly during remote operation under PC control—ensure a stable 5V, 1A power supply, compliant with USB 3.0 or USB Power Delivery (USB-PD) specifications. Powering the device with only 5V, 0.5A (typical of USB 2.0) may result in unreliable performance, including failure to power on or disruptions in motion tracking.
documents.tags: Neurotechnology, Commutator Pro, DC Jack, USB C Port, Motion Tracking, Power Delivery USB PD, USB 3.0 Specifications, Neuroprosthetics, FAE Engineer, Power Supply Requirements

documents.title: Commutator PRO, Battery charging
documents.text: If your Commutator Pro is equipped with an internal battery (models designated with -B), the battery will begin charging automatically when power is connected. During charging, the front-panel battery level indicator LEDs will flash and illuminate progressively to reflect the current charge level, indicating active charging (see LED Indicators section for details).

documents.text: The Commutator Pro is designed to provide reliable power switching in high-speed data transmission systems such as NP serial transmit applications. When operating with GMSL differential signals, users must take precautions to avoid electromagnetic interference (EMI) that may lead to errors during PRBS or serial transmission testing.

documents.title: Standard Commutator HDMI
documents.text: The Standard Commutator HDMI is a passive, low-torque commutator designed for KonteX X-Headstage™ systems. It enables continuous mechanical rotation while maintaining stable electrical connections, minimizing cable twisting and mechanical stress during freely moving animal experiments. The commutator is compatible with XR headstages (up to 256 channels) and XSR headstages (up to 32 channels) using an HDMI-based interface.
documents.tags: Neurotechnology devices, FAE technical documents, Commutator components, HDMI interfaces, Headstage systems, Electrophysiology equipment, Neuroscience research tools, Brain-machine interface technology, Neural recording hardware, KonteX X-Headstage commutators.

documents.text: This commutator is intended for use in freely moving or rotating experimental setups, where continuous rotation is required without compromising signal continuity or introducing additional electrical noise.

documents.title: Standard Commutator HDMI, Assembly Process
documents.text:
① Mounting the Commutator
Securely mount the Standard Commutator HDMI (Commutator STD) above the behavior box or experimental arena. Ensure the commutator is firmly fixed and aligned vertically to allow smooth, unrestricted rotation during operation.
documents.tags: HDMI Comutator, Neurotechnology Hardware Documentation, FAE Support, Commutator Mounting Procedure, Assembly Process Guidance, Standard Commutator Specifications, Electrical Interface Design, Rotation Mechanism, Vertical Alignment Requirements, Secure Fastening Methods

documents.title: Standard Commutator HDMI, Assembly Process
documents.text:
② Headstage-to-Commutator Connection
Using a compatible HDMI cable, connect the X-Headstage to the Commutator STD. Verify that the connectors are fully seated and properly aligned before proceeding.
documents.tags: Neurotechnology, FAE Expertise, Standard Commutator HDMI, Assembly Process, Headstage-to-Commutator Connection, HDMI Cable Connections, Neurological Interface Hardware, Commutator Connectivity Solutions, Advanced Electroencephalography (EEG) Systems, Electrophysiology Signal Processing

documents.title: Standard Commutator HDMI, Assembly Process
documents.text:
③ Cable Routing and Strain Management
Route the cable according to the illustrated configuration, wrapping and securing it around the rotating shaft of the Commutator STD as indicated. Leave sufficient cable length to accommodate the animal’s full range of motion while minimizing excess slack that could introduce mechanical drag or tangling.
documents.tags: HDMI commutator standard assembly, Neurotechnology assembly process, FAE assembly instructions, Cable routing for HDMI commutators, Strain management techniques, Rotating shaft cable attachment, Commutator STD assembly procedures, Animal motion range consideration, Mechanical drag minimization methods, Cable slack reduction strategies.

documents.title: Standard Commutator HDMI, Assembly Process
documents.text:
④ Final Headstage Connection
Connect the opposite end of the HDMI cable to the X-Headstage. Confirm that the connection is secure and that the cable routing does not impose axial or lateral forces on the headstage connector.
documents.tags: HDMI, Headstage Connector, Neurotechnology, FAE Expert, Commutator Assembly, Sectional Connection, Axial Force, Lateral Force, Cable Routing, Secure Connection

documents.title: Standard Commutator HDMI, Assembly Process
documents.text:
⑤ Commutator-to-XDAQ Connection
Connect the fixed (non-rotating) end of the Commutator STD to the XDAQ acquisition system using a compatible HDMI cable. Ensure the connection is secure and that the cable is properly strain-relieved.
NOTE:
1. Mount the commutator in a vertical orientation to ensure optimal rotational performance.
2. Avoid excessive axial or lateral forces on the HDMI connectors.
3. Ensure sufficient cable slack to allow free rotation without tension.
documents.tags: HDMI connector, Commutator STD assembly, XDAQ acquisition system, Neurotechnology hardware, FAE technical documentation, Technical specification, Assembly instructions, Rotational performance, Connector strain relief

documents.title: Adpt-X6-EXT
documents.text: The X6 Cycle Extender (SKU: Adpt-X6-EXT) is a disposable interface adapter designed for use with X6 headstages. It provides a sacrificial connection point that helps reduce mechanical wear and tear on the X6 headstage connector from repeated mating cycles. This accessory prolongs the usable life of the expensive headstage connector by absorbing the connection cycles instead of the headstage itself.

documents.title: Adpt-X3-EXT
documents.text: The X3 Cycle Extender is a disposable adapter designed for use with KonteX X3 headstages. It serves as a protective intermediary between the headstage and connected accessories, reducing mechanical wear on the headstage connector caused by frequent mating and unmating cycles. By absorbing connector wear, the Cycle Extender helps prolong the operational lifespan of X3 headstages.

documents.text: The Adpt-Omnetics series is designed to interface KonteX X3R headstages with probes using a support 16-, 32-, and 64-channel Omnetics connector's probe. Its design allows users to conveniently swap different types of microelectrode array chips based on specific experimental requirements.

documents.title: Adpt-MEA2, Device Overview
documents.text: In vitro experiments often struggle to replicate the cellular conditions found in living organisms. Primary cells cultured on rigid multi-electrode arrays (MEAs) exhibit significant differences from their in vivo counterparts, limiting the predictive value of such data. MEAs utilize glass multi-electrode arrays to recreate the mechanical and electrical environments of in vivo cells in a controlled setting. They can be paired with various headstages and stimulation array boards for optical

documents.title: Adpt-OM16
documents.text: The Adpt-Omnetics series is designed to interface KonteX X3(S)R headstages with probes using a support 16-, 32-, and 64-channel Omnetics connector's probe. Available in three configurations— standard with screw-locking for secure attachment, no-lock for a reduced footprint, and vertical to provide additional clearance near the craniotomy—these adapters enable flexible integration into diverse experimental setups while maintaining a compact and reliable connection.
documents.tags: lets a different connector standard work on the standard headstage family, cross-connector-type adaptation for the primary product line

documents.title: Adpt-OM32
documents.text: The Adpt-Omnetics series is designed to interface KonteX X3R headstages with probes using a support 16-, 32-, and 64-channel Omnetics connector's probe. Available in three configurations— standard with screw-locking for secure attachment, no-lock for a reduced footprint, and vertical to provide additional clearance near the craniotomy—these adapters enable flexible integration into diverse experimental setups while maintaining a compact and reliable connection.

documents.title: Adpt-SRRemap
documents.text: The SR Headstage Remapper is designed specifically for use with the X3SR32 headstage. It enables compatibility with standard probe adapters that lack the "SR" (Stim-Record) designation. This adapter enables users to connect SR-type headstages to a wider range of compatible adapters and also functions as a headstage cycle extender.
documents.tags: adapts a headstage so it can use accessories it wasn't originally designed for, cross-compatibility bridge between mismatched hardware generations

documents.title: XDAQ
documents.text: XDAQ delivers seamless, native integration with Neuropixels 1.0 and 2.0, supporting high-density recordings of up to 4 probes and 3,072 channels per system. Its high-speed Thunderbolt interface ensures powerful, low-latency performance for real-time analysis and closed-loop neuroscience applications.
documents.tags: Neuropixels integration, XDAQ software, Neurotechnology solutions, High-density recording hardware, FAE support, Neural interface technology, Closed-loop neurostimulation, Real-time data acquisition, Low-latency Thunderbolt interface, Neuroscience application development, in vivo calcium imaging of mouse hippocampus

documents.title: XDAQ
documents.text: Fully compatible with standard imec headstages and cables, XDAQ eliminates the need for QBSC cards and PXI enclosures—simplifying setup and reducing system complexity. The XDAQ AIO offers all the functionality of OneBox, while delivering expanded performance and enhanced flexibility for advanced research needs.
documents.tags: XDAQ, imec headstages, XDAQ AIO, neurotechnology, FAE, technical documentation, product integration, PXI enclosures, QBSC cards, analog-to-digital conversion.

documents.title: XDAQ
documents.text: As the top-tier model in the XDAQ series, the AIO sets a new standard for versatility and capability in neuroscience instrumentation, offering robust native support for large-scale neural recording, optogenetic stimulation, and synchronized video acquisition. It supports up to 1024 channels of high-resolution spike recordings and, in XSR mode, provides up to 128 channels that can be dynamically configured for recording or stimulation. It also delivers seamless compatibility with advanced
documents.tags: one unit that covers multiple product tiers' functionality, flagship model spanning several use cases

documents.title: XDAQ
documents.text: electrode technologies—including Neuropixels probes—with the ability to operate up to four probes simultaneously for high-density, multi-region neural interrogation.
documents.tags: XDAQ, Neural Interrogation, Electrode Technologies, Neuropixels Probes, High-Density Recording, Multi-Region Neural Interfaces, Neural Probe Technology, Brain-Computer Interface, Neurotechnology Solutions, Neural Data Acquisition

documents.title: XDAQ
documents.text: The ONE+ model is a uniquely versatile platform that unifies multiple capabilities into a single device. It supports up to 1024 channels of high-resolution spike recordings and, in XSR mode, provides up to 128 channels that can be dynamically configured for recording or stimulation. Additionally, the system includes native support for advanced electrode technologies, such as Neuropixels probes, with the capacity to operate up to four probes simultaneously.
documents.tags: mode can be reconfigured in software rather than requiring new hardware, single device covering multiple operating modes

documents.title: XDAQ
documents.text: The CORE2 model is a uniquely versatile platform that unifies multiple capabilities into a single device. It supports up to 512 channels of high-resolution spike recordings and, in XSR mode, provides up to 32 channels that can be dynamically configured for recording or stimulation. Additionally, the system includes native support for advanced electrode technologies, such as Neuropixels probes, with the capacity to operate up to two probes simultaneously.
documents.tags: Neurotechnology hardware, FAE expert, XDAQ documentation, CORE2 model, high-resolution spike recordings, multi-channel neural interface, electrode technologies, Neuropixels probe compatibility, neural stimulation system, advanced neuroscience instrumentation.

documents.title: XDAQ AIO
documents.text: The XDAQ™ product line offers a suite of acquisition solutions designed to address the evolving needs of modern neuroscience research. These systems combine flexibility and performance, enabling integrated, multimodal approaches to a wide range of experimental techniques.
documents.tags: Neurotechnology, FAE Expertise, XDAQ Acquisition Solutions, Multimodal Data Acquisition, Neuroscience Research Equipment, Advanced Data Analytics, Neurosignal Processing, Experimental Techniques, Brain Function Measurement

documents.title: XDAQ AIO
documents.text: As the top-tier model in the XDAQ series, the AIO sets a new standard for versatility and capability in neuroscience instrumentation, offering robust native support for large-scale neural recording, optogenetic stimulation, and synchronized video acquisition. It supports up to 1024 channels of high-resolution spike recordings and, in XSR mode, provides up to 128 channels that can be dynamically configured for recording or stimulation. It also delivers seamless compatibility with advanced
documents.tags: one unit that covers multiple product tiers' functionality, flagship model spanning several use cases

documents.title: XDAQ AIO
documents.text: electrode technologies—including Neuropixels probes—with the ability to operate up to four probes simultaneously for high-density, multi-region neural interrogation.
documents.tags: Neurotechnology, AIO, XDAQ, Anti-Imaging-Occlusion, Multi-Probe, Nano-electrodes, Fine Electrode Array, High-Density Neural Interface, Neural Interrogation Probe Management

documents.title: XDAQ AIO
documents.text: With its high-bandwidth architecture, extensive I/O options, and precision timing synchronization, the AIO is purpose-built for real-time, closed-loop experiments, multimodal fusion, and large-scale parallel data acquisition across complex behavioral paradigms.
documents.tags: Xdaq Aio Architecture, Real-Time Data Acquisition, Multimodal Fusion Platform, High-Bandwidth I/O System, Closed-Loop Experimentation, Precision Timing Synchronization, Large-Scale Parallel Processing, Behavioral Paradigm Analysis, Neurotechnology Hardware, Advanced Input Output Systems

documents.title: XDAQ AIO, Setting Up The IO Ports
documents.text:
① Analog BNC Ports
AIO features two pairs of wide range (±10V), general purpose 16-bit ADC (AI-01, AI-02) and DAC (AO-01, AO-02) channels, each equipped with a BNC connector for easy access. These ports operate on the same sample rate as the X-Headstage™ (XR, XSR Mode). In NP mode, the sample rate is fixed at 30kHz.
documents.tags: XDAQ AIO setup, Analog IO ports, BNC connectors, ADC channels, DAC channels, 16-bit resolution, Wide range input, AO-01, AO-02, AI-01, AI-02

documents.title: XDAQ AIO, Setting Up The IO Ports
documents.text:
② Digital BNC Ports
AIO features two pairs of digital input (DI-01, DI-02) and output (DO-01, DO-02) channels, each equipped with a BNC connector. The digital inputs support both 3.3V and 5V digital signals. The signal level for the BNC digital output ports can be configured via software to either 3.3V or 5V logic high. The digital I/Os operate at the same sample rate as the X-Headstage™ (XR, XSR mode). In NP mode, the sample rate is fixed at 30 kHz.
⚠
IMPORTANT:
Do not connect ports to unintended signal type. Permanet damage may occour.
documents.tags: Neurotechnology hardware documentation, AIO digital I/O configuration, XDAQ analog input output settings, FAE expert support, digital BNC port troubleshooting, hardware setup guidelines, X-Headstage compatibility issues, sample rate configuration options, analog digital interface requirements.

documents.title: XDAQ AIO, Setting Up The IO Ports
documents.text:
③ Thor Vision USB Port
XDAQ AIO features a on-board hardware video processor capable of capturing up to four UVC camera streams simultaneously, or a single high-speed camera stream at up to 300 FPS. Each video frame is precisely time-stamped with the corresponding neural data acquisition timestamp.
By utilizing a dedicated hardware video encoding engine, the system ensures reliable, lossless frame capture—eliminating dropped or skipped frames commonly associated with software-based solutions.
documents.tags: Neurotechnology, FAE Expert, XDAQ AIO, IO Ports Setup, Thor Vision USB Port, UVC Camera Streams, High-Speed Camera Capture, Hardware Video Encoding, Time-Stamped Frame Capture, Neural Data Acquisition

documents.title: XDAQ AIO, Setting Up The IO Ports
documents.text:
④ LED Driver Ports
XDAQ AIO features 4ch of onboard high-power light source drivers. Drive LED light sources from commercial vendors like Thorlab, Plexon, TDT etc.
Stimulus intensity and modulation can be controlled independently of the main neural acquisition.Each light source driver supports up to 1A of power output and modulation frequency from DC to 10kHz.
documents.tags: each output channel independently controllable, per-channel brightness/intensity software control, multi-channel light source driver

documents.title: XDAQ AIO, Setting Up The IO Ports
documents.text:
⑤ DB25 IO Port
The DB25 connector provides six additional pairs of digital I/O channels. The digital inputs on the DB25 connector (DI-03 to DI-08) are compatible with both 3.3V and 5V digital signals. The digital outputs on the DB25 connector (DO-03 to DO-08) are designed for 3.3V logic high. The digital I/Os operate at the same sample rate as the X-Headstage™ (XR, XSR mode). The connector also includes supplementary device signals for interfacing with external devices.
documents.tags: Neurotechnology, FAE, XDAQ AIO, IO Ports, DB25 Connector, Digital I/O Channels, 3.3V Logic High, Sample Rate, External Device Interface

documents.title: XDAQ AIO, Setting Up The IO Ports
documents.text:
⑥ Ground Ports
Sys GND —
the digital ground shared by the internal electronic circuitry.
Chassis GND —
connected to the metal enclosure, the outer shield of the HDMI cable, and earth ground via the power supply.
As a starting point, we recommend bonding the two ground ports together and using this common node to ground the Faraday cage shielding. Since environmental conditions can vary significantly, users should test and adjust the grounding configuration to determine the most effective setup for their specific environment.
documents.tags: Xdaq AIO IO ports settings, Faraday cage shielding ground, Grounding configurations for electronics, Chassis GND vs Sys GND, Digital ground connection, Earth grounding methods, Electronic circuitry grounding, Shielding design considerations, Electrostatic discharge protection, Noise reduction strategies

documents.title: XDAQ AIO, Setting Up The IO Ports
documents.text:
⑦ Expansion Ports
The EXP1 and EXP2 ports connect to XDAQ™ accessories, such as the I/O Expander, to provide additional digital and analog I/O functionality.
documents.tags: xdaq aio hardware documentation, io expansion modules, xdaq accessories, i/o expander module, analog io ports, digital io ports, expansion ports settings, xdaq io configuration, neurotechnology devices

documents.title: XDAQ AIO, Setting Up The IO Ports
documents.text:
⑧ 3.5mm Audio Port
This 3.5mm stereo jack enables audio monitoring of two selected headstage amplifier channels, configured via software: Analog Out 1 (AO-01), which is routed to the left audio channel, and Analog Out 2 (AO-02), which is routed to the right audio channel.
⚠
IMPORTANT:
Do not connect ports to unintended signal type. Permanet damage may occour.
documents.tags: Neurotechnology hardware documentation, XDAQ AIO IO port setup, Audio monitoring headstage amplifier channels, Analog Out configuration, 3.5mm stereo jack usage, Signal routing software configuration, Headstage amplifier audio output, Neurological signal processing hardware, Audio interface configuration settings

documents.text: While Thunderbolt and USB-C share the same physical connector, they are not interchangeable. Thunderbolt offers significantly higher data transfer rates and requires specific compatible cables. Retrofitting a PC with a Thunderbolt add-on card is generally impractical, as these cards are model-specific and often incompatible with other systems. To prevent compatibility issues, users should verify that their PC includes native Thunderbolt support.

documents.title: XDAQ CORE2
documents.text: The XDAQ™ product line offers a suite of acquisition solutions designed to address the evolving needs of modern neuroscience research. These systems combine flexibility and performance, enabling integrated, multimodal approaches to a wide range of experimental techniques.
documents.tags: neurotechnology hardware, xdaq core2 system, neuroscience data acquisition, brain research equipment, fae interface, xdaq suite solutions, neuroacquisition software, high-performance computing, neural recording systems, neurodata management.

documents.title: XDAQ CORE2
documents.text: The CORE2 model is a uniquely versatile platform that unifies multiple capabilities into a single device. It supports up to 512 channels of high-resolution spike recordings and, in XSR mode, provides up to 32 channels that can be dynamically configured for recording or stimulation. Additionally, the system includes native support for advanced electrode technologies, such as Neuropixels probes, with the capacity to operate up to two probes simultaneously.
documents.tags: Neurotechnology, FAE Expertise, XDAQ CORE2, High-Resolution Spike Recordings, Neural Stimulation, Electrode Technologies, Neuropixels Probes, Neuroprosthetics, Advanced Brain Machine Interfaces, Multifunctional Neural Devices

documents.title: XDAQ CORE2, Setting Up The IO Ports
documents.text:
① Analog BNC Ports
CORE2 features a set of wide range (±10V), general purpose 16-bit ADC (AI-01) and DAC (AO-01) channels, each equipped with a BNC connector for easy access. These ports operate on the same sample rate as the X-Headstage™ (XR, XSR Mode). In NP mode, the sample rate is fixed at 30kHz.
documents.tags: xdaq core2 analog bnc port settings, xdaq core2 io configuration, dac ao01 channels, adc ai01 channels, xdaq core2 hardware setup, analog input output ports, xdaq core2 sampling rates, neurotechnology io ports, fae system integration

documents.title: XDAQ CORE2, Setting Up The IO Ports
documents.text:
② Digital BNC Ports
CORE2 features a set of digital input (DI-01) and output (DO-01) channels, each equipped with a BNC connector. The digital inputs support both 3.3V and 5V digital signals. The signal level for the BNC digital output ports can be configured via software to either 3.3V or 5V logic high. The digital I/Os operate at the same sample rate as the X-Headstage™ (XR, XSR mode). In NP mode, the sample rate is fixed at 30 kHz.
⚠
IMPORTANT:
Do not connect ports to unintended signal type. Permanet damage may occour.
documents.tags: Neurotechnology hardware documentation, digital IO settings, BNC connector configuration, XDAQ CORE2 setup, FAE expertise, digital signal levels, sample rate configurations, X-Headstage integration, IO port safety precautions

documents.title: XDAQ CORE2, Setting Up The IO Ports
documents.text:
③ DB25 IO Port
The DB25 connector provides six additional pairs of digital I/O channels. The digital inputs on the DB25 connector (DI-03 to DI-08) are compatible with both 3.3V and 5V digital signals. The digital outputs on the DB25 connector (DO-03 to DO-08) are designed for 3.3V logic high. The digital I/Os operate at the same sample rate as the X-Headstage™ (XR, XSR mode). The connector also includes supplementary device signals for interfacing with external devices.
documents.tags: extra digital channels reachable through an auxiliary connector, supplementary IO beyond the main port set

documents.title: XDAQ CORE2, Setting Up The IO Ports
documents.text:
④ Ground Ports
Sys GND —
the digital ground shared by the internal electronic circuitry.
Chassis GND —
connected to the metal enclosure, the outer shield of the HDMI cable, and earth ground via the power supply.
As a starting point, we recommend bonding the two ground ports together and using this common node to ground the Faraday cage shielding. Since environmental conditions can vary significantly, users should test and adjust the grounding configuration to determine the most effective setup for their specific environment.
documents.tags: Xdaq core2 io port setup, digital ground reference, chassis ground connection, faraday cage shielding, electronic circuitry grounding, low frequency noise reduction, electromagnetic interference mitigation, system grounding configuration, ground plane bonding, noise suppression techniques.

documents.title: XDAQ CORE2, Setting Up The IO Ports
documents.text:
⑤ Expansion Ports
The EXP1 and EXP2 ports connect to XDAQ™ accessories, such as the I/O Expander, to provide additional digital and analog I/O functionality.
documents.tags: xdaq-core2, i-o-expansion-ports, exp1-port, exp2-port, io-expander-accessory, xdaq-acc-ports, expansion-card-io, daq-configuration, xdaq-hardware-documentation, i-o-functionality

documents.title: XDAQ CORE2, Setting Up The IO Ports
documents.text:
⑥ 3.5mm Audio Port
This 3.5mm stereo jack enables audio monitoring of two selected headstage amplifier channels, configured via software: Analog Out 1 (AO-01), which is routed to the left audio channel, and Analog Out 2 (AO-02), which is routed to the right audio channel.
⚠
IMPORTANT:
Do not connect ports to unintended signal type. Permanet damage may occour.
documents.tags: Neurotechnology hardware documentation, FAE expert advice, XDAQ CORE2 technical specifications, IO port configuration, 3.5mm audio jack setup, Headstage amplifier channels routing, Analog Out 1 and 2 configuration, Audio monitoring in neuro devices, Neurostimulation equipment settings.

documents.title: XDAQ CORE2, Reference PC configuration
documents.text: While Thunderbolt and USB-C share the same physical connector, they are not interchangeable. Thunderbolt offers significantly higher data transfer rates and requires specific compatible cables. Retrofitting a PC with a Thunderbolt add-on card is generally impractical, as these cards are model-specific and often incompatible with other systems. To prevent compatibility issues, users should verify that their PC includes native Thunderbolt support.

documents.title: XDAQ ONE+
documents.text: The XDAQ™ product line offers a suite of acquisition solutions designed to address the evolving needs of modern neuroscience research. These systems combine flexibility and performance, enabling integrated, multimodal approaches to a wide range of experimental techniques.
documents.tags: Neurotechnology hardware, FAE Support, XDAQ ONE+, Data Acquisition Systems, Multimodal Neuroscience Research, Experimental Techniques, Neuroscientific Instrumentation, Hardware Documentation, Technical Specifications, Neurotech Expertise

documents.title: XDAQ ONE+
documents.text: The ONE+ model is a uniquely versatile platform that unifies multiple capabilities into a single device. It supports up to 1024 channels of high-resolution spike recordings and, in XSR mode, provides up to 128 channels that can be dynamically configured for recording or stimulation. Additionally, the system includes native support for advanced electrode technologies, such as Neuropixels probes, with the capacity to operate up to four probes simultaneously.
documents.tags: mode can be reconfigured in software rather than requiring new hardware, single device covering multiple operating modes

documents.title: XDAQ ONE+
documents.text: With comprehensive onboard and expandable input/output (I/O) capabilities, the XDAQ™ seamlessly integrates with standard laboratory equipments, ensuring compatibility across diverse experimental setups.

documents.title: XDAQ ONE+, Setting Up The IO Ports
documents.text:
① Analog BNC Ports
ONE+ features two pairs of wide range (±10V), general purpose 16-bit ADC (AI-01, AI-02) and DAC (AO-01, AO-02) channels, each equipped with a BNC connector for easy access. These ports operate on the same sample rate as the X-Headstage™ (XR, XSR Mode). In NP mode, the sample rate is fixed at 30kHz.
documents.tags: accepts a broad range of external analog signal levels, flexible input range for diverse third-party sensors

documents.title: XDAQ ONE+, Setting Up The IO Ports
documents.text:
② Digital BNC Ports
ONE+ features two pairs of digital input (DI-01, DI-02) and output (DO-01, DO-02) channels, each equipped with a BNC connector. The digital inputs support both 3.3V and 5V digital signals. The signal level for the BNC digital output ports can be configured via software to either 3.3V or 5V logic high. The digital I/Os operate at the same sample rate as the X-Headstage™ (XR, XSR mode). In NP mode, the sample rate is fixed at 30 kHz.
⚠
IMPORTANT:
Do not connect ports to unintended signal type. Permanet damage may occour.
documents.tags: Neurotechnology hardware documentation, Digital BNC Ports settings, XDAQ ONE+ setup, IO Port configuration, Digital Input Output channels, High-speed data acquisition, Sample rate synchronization, 3.3V and 5V digital signal support, FPGA-based I/O control

documents.title: XDAQ ONE+, Setting Up The IO Ports
documents.text:
③ DB25 IO Port
The DB25 connector provides six additional pairs of digital I/O channels. The digital inputs on the DB25 connector (DI-03 to DI-08) are compatible with both 3.3V and 5V digital signals. The digital outputs on the DB25 connector (DO-03 to DO-08) are designed for 3.3V logic high. The digital I/Os operate at the same sample rate as the X-Headstage™ (XR, XSR mode). The connector also includes supplementary device signals for interfacing with external devices.
documents.tags: extra digital channels reachable through an auxiliary connector, supplementary IO beyond the main port set

documents.title: XDAQ ONE+, Setting Up The IO Ports
documents.text:
④ Ground Ports
Sys GND —
the digital ground shared by the internal electronic circuitry.
Chassis GND —
connected to the metal enclosure, the outer shield of the HDMI cable, and earth ground via the power supply.
As a starting point, we recommend bonding the two ground ports together and using this common node to ground the Faraday cage shielding. Since environmental conditions can vary significantly, users should test and adjust the grounding configuration to determine the most effective setup for their specific environment.
documents.tags: Neurotechnology, FAE Expertise, IO Port Configuration, Grounding Techniques, Electronic Circuitry, Shielding Methods, Faraday Cage Design, Grounding Strategies, System Setup Guidelines, Electrostatic Discharge Protection

documents.title: XDAQ ONE+, Setting Up The IO Ports
documents.text:

The EXP1 and EXP2 ports connect to XDAQ™ accessories, such as the I/O Expander, to provide additional digital and analog I/O functionality.
documents.tags: neurotechnology hardware, fae expert, xdaq one plus settings, io port configuration, expansion ports, analog io functionality, digital io expansion, input output configuration, xdaq accessories integration

documents.title: XDAQ ONE+, Setting Up The IO Ports
documents.text:
⑥ 3.5mm Audio Port
This 3.5mm stereo jack enables audio monitoring of two selected headstage amplifier channels, configured via software: Analog Out 1 (AO-01), which is routed to the left audio channel, and Analog Out 2 (AO-02), which is routed to the right audio channel.
⚠
IMPORTANT:
Do not connect ports to unintended signal type. Permanet damage may occour.

documents.title: XDAQ ONE+, Reference PC configuration
documents.text: While Thunderbolt and USB-C share the same physical connector, they are not interchangeable. Thunderbolt offers significantly higher data transfer rates and requires specific compatible cables. Retrofitting a PC with a Thunderbolt add-on card is generally impractical, as these cards are model-specific and often incompatible with other systems. To prevent compatibility issues, users should verify that their PC includes native Thunderbolt support.
documents.tags: Thunderbolt 3 specification, USB-C cable compatibility, High-speed data transfer technology, FAE expert troubleshooting, Neurotechnology hardware configuration, Reference PC setup requirements, Interchangeable connector limitations, Model-specific hardware upgrade challenges, Data transmission rate comparison.

documents.title: XDAQ ONE+, XDAQ™ DB25 IO Connector Pinout
documents.text: All digital inputs (DI-01 to DI-08) are compatible with both 3.3V and 5V digital signals. The BNC digital output ports (DO-01, DO-02) can be configured via software to output either a 3.3V or 5V logic high signal. The digital output channels on the DB25 connector (DO-03 to DO-08) are designed for 3.3V logic high output.

documents.title: Tester-Stim-X3
documents.text: The X3 Headstage Stimulation Test Module is designed to verify the stimulus waveform generated by X3SR headstages. By connecting the module to a dummy 1 kΩ resistive load, users can safely observe and validate stimulation output characteristics without connecting to biological tissue.

documents.title: Tester-Stim-X3, Test Operation
documents.text: Due to the keyed connector pin orientation, the X3 Headstage supports left- and right-side connections. Users should alternate the connection orientation to verify stimulation waveforms on each side, ensuring complete testing while preventing incorrect insertion or electrical misalignment.

documents.title: Cable-IMU
documents.text: The Commutator IMU Cable is a dedicated communication cable designed to interface an external Inertial Measurement Unit (IMU) sensor with the KonteX Commutator system. The cable serves as the communication link that enables real-time transmission of motion and orientation data from the IMU sensor to the Commutator controller.
documents.tags: KonteX Commutator, Inertial Measurement Unit Cable, IMU Sensor Interface, Commutator IMU Cable, Motion Data Transmission, Orientation Data Transmission, Real-Time IMU Integration, FMCW Radar Interface, Neurotechnology Communication Cable, Advanced IMU Connectivity.

documents.title: Cable-IMU
documents.text: During freely moving experiments, animals may continuously rotate, causing tethered cables to twist and accumulate torque. The IMU sensor monitors the animal’s rotational movement using gyroscopic measurements and transmits this information through the IMU Cable to the Commutator. Based on the detected direction and magnitude of rotation, the Commutator automatically rotates in the corresponding direction, preventing cable twisting and maintaining a low-torque environment throughout the
documents.tags: IMU sensor calibration, wireless animal tracking systems, rotational movement measurement, gyroscopic sensing technology, animal locomotion monitoring, tethered animal experiments, IMU cable management, commutator rotation mechanism, torque reduction systems.

documents.text: experiment.

documents.title: Cable-IMU
documents.text: By actively compensating for rotational motion, the Commutator IMU system helps preserve natural animal behavior, reduces mechanical stress on implants and cables, and improves the reliability of long-duration recording sessions.
documents.tags: Commutator IMU System, Animal Behavior Tracking, Rotational Motion Compensation, Implantable Sensors, Neurotechnology Hardware, Cable Protection, FAE Expertise, Reliability Improvement, Long-Duration Recording, IMU Calibration

documents.title: Tester-Z-X3
documents.text: The X3 Headstage 64-Channel Impedance Test Module is designed to evaluate the impedance measurement functionality of X3 headstages. By connecting the module to dummy test loads with resistive and capacitive (R/C) values ranging from kΩ to MΩ, users can verify impedance measurement accuracy without connecting to electrodes or biological tissue.
documents.tags: Neurotechnology, Headstage, Impedance Test Module, FAE Support, Technical Documentation, X3 Tester, Neurostimulation, Electrophysiology Equipment, Medical Device Testing, Impedance Measurement, measuring impedance of implanted electrodes before surgery

documents.title: Tester-Z-X3, Test Operation
documents.text: Due to the keyed orientation of the connector pins, impedance testing must be performed in left and right orientations separately. Users should alternate the connection orientation of the X3 Headstage 64-Channel Impedance Test Module to verify impedance measurements on both sides, ensuring complete and accurate validation.
documents.tags: Neurotechnology, FAE Expertise, Impedance Testing, Multi-Orientation Tester, High-Channel Density Module, Electrode Interface Technology, Connector Pin Orientation, Validation Procedures, Biopotential Measurement Tools, Neurophysiology Research Equipment

documents.text: The HDMI A to D Cable (3M) is a high-quality data transmission cable designed to connect devices with a standard HDMI Type-A port to equipment requiring a HDMI Type-D (Micro HDMI) interface.
